title: Circadian Light & Sleep Optimization
hook_question: Can dim screen glow after sunset confuse our ancient biological clocks?
takeaway_pill: Exposing eyes to bright sunlight within 30 minutes of waking and eliminating artificial blue light after dark aligns hormone release for deeper restorative sleep.
content: Our **circadian rhythm** is dictated by the suprachiasmatic nucleus, which relies on light cues to synchronize peripheral organs. Morning light resets the master clock, stopping the production of **melatonin** and starting cortisol synthesis. Throughout the day, waking activities cause **adenosine** to build up in the basal forebrain, creating sleep pressure. Artificial light exposure at night blocks melatonin secretion, preventing the deep and REM cycles necessary for neurological recovery and hormone synthesis.

evidence_table.outcome: Brief pulses of bright morning light shifted circadian phase by up to 2 hours.
E-readers before bed suppressed melatonin by 50% and delayed sleep onset by 10 minutes.

bibliography.text: Duffy JF, et al. Effect of Light on Human Circadian Physiology. Sleep Med Clin. 2009;4(2):165-177.
